topics.title: Review of drafting software: Plan a single storeyed residential building with flat roof for given requirements and draw the site plan, plan, section and elevation
topics.content:
> **APJ ABDUL KALAM TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY (KTU) | 2024 SCHEME**
> - **Course:** PCCVL507 - CIVIL ENGINEERING MODELLING LAB
> - **Module:** Module Experiment 2
> - **Topic:** Review of drafting software: Plan a single storeyed residential building with flat roof for given requirements and draw the site plan, plan, section and elevation

<!-- SECTION_1_START -->

# 1. Core Technical Definition & Intuitive Overview

## 1.1 Formal Academic Definition

**Building Drafting and Planning** is the systematic process of preparing scaled graphical representations of a proposed residential structure using Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) or Building Information Modelling (BIM) software, in strict conformance with the National Building Code of India (NBC 2016), Kerala Municipal Building Rules (KMBR / KPBR), and KTU 2024 Scheme drafting conventions. For a single-storeyed flat-roof residential unit, the deliverable consists of **four primary drawings**: the **Site Plan**, the **Floor Plan**, the **Cross-Section**, and the **Front Elevation**.

A **flat roof** in KTU context refers to a roof slab with a finished slope of **1:100 to 1:80** (towards the drain) and a parapet wall of minimum **900 mm** above the roof level for residential occupancies as per IS 875 (Part 3) and KMBR Clause 41.

> [!NOTE]
> **KTU 2024 Syllabus Highlight (PCCVL507 – Module 2)**
> The experiment tests three competencies: (i) selection of appropriate drafting tool, (ii) translation of textual building requirements into a code-compliant plan, and (iii) generation of orthographic projection set (plan + section + elevation) at standard scales.

## 1.2 Standard Scales Mandated by KTU / BIS

| Drawing Type | Standard Scale (BIS 962) | KTU Exam Expectation |
|---|---|---|
| Site Plan | 1 : 200 | Always show North arrow & plot boundary |
| Floor Plan | 1 : 50 or 1 : 100 | Furnished layout with dimensions |
| Cross-Section | 1 : 50 | Pass through staircase, doors, windows |
| Elevation | 1 : 50 or 1 : 100 | Show all openings, parapet, finishes |
| Detailed Section (through stair / WC) | 1 : 20 or 1 : 10 | Optional, marks extra credit |

## 1.3 Conceptual Analogy — "The House is a Tailor's Master Pattern"

Imagine you walk into a master tailor's shop and ask for a custom three-piece suit. The tailor does **not** start cutting cloth. He first measures your body (the *site survey*), then draws a paper pattern on a flat table (*plan drawing*), cuts a muslin mock-up to test the fit (*section view* to verify headroom and clearances), and finally shows you how the suit will look on you from the front (*elevation view*). The drafting software (AutoCAD / Revit) is the tailor's table — it lets the engineer "cut the house in paper" before a single brick is laid, ensuring every door swings, every window aligns, and every pipe slope works.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> A plan is **not** a picture of the house — it is a **horizontal slice** taken at sill level (typically **900 mm** above the finished floor) showing what you would see if the roof were removed and you looked straight down. This is the single most misunderstood concept in KTU viva voce.

## 1.4 Review of Drafting Software — Tool Classification

The KTU 2024 lab manual lists the following software categories as permissible for this experiment:

- **2D CAD** — AutoCAD 2024, DraftSight, LibreCAD (for the orthographic drawing set).
- **3D BIM / Modelling** — Autodesk Revit 2024, ArchiCAD 26, SketchUp Pro (for massing study and 3D walkthrough).
- **Rendering / Presentation** — Lumion 2024, Twinmotion, V-Ray (for the elevation visualisation, optional).
- **Open-Source Alternatives** — FreeCAD, Blender, QCAD — accepted in lab if licensed AutoCAD is unavailable.

The **recommended primary tool** for this experiment is **AutoCAD 2024** (or any LT/equivalent release) because KTU board evaluation focuses on command-line drafting skill, layer management, and the ability to produce dimensioned 2D sheets on standard A1/A2 layouts.

> [!TIP]
> **Minimum System Specification for AutoCAD 2024** (for reference only — students need not memorise):
> CPU ≥ Intel i5 10th Gen, RAM ≥ **16 GB**, GPU ≥ **4 GB VRAM**, SSD ≥ **512 GB**. The lab is provided with pre-configured workstations.

<!-- SECTION_1_END -->

<!-- SECTION_2_START -->

# 2. Deep Theoretical Analysis & KTU High-Yield Formula Sheet

## 2.1 Hierarchical Logic of Building Drawing Production

The production of the four drawing sheets follows a strict hierarchical pipeline. Skipping a stage produces an un-evaluable drawing.

- **Stage 1 — Requirements Intake (Brief Stage)**
  - Plot size, frontage, road width, number of bedrooms, Vastu orientation, parking, staircase type.
  - Output: *Design Brief Document*.

- **Stage 2 — Code Compliance Check**
  - Verify setbacks (front, rear, side), FAR, ground coverage, height, parking as per KMBR / KPBR / Panchayat building rules.
  - Output: *Compliance Sheet* (in table form).

- **Stage 3 — Bubble Diagram / Zoning**
  - Group rooms into Public (drawing, dining, verandah), Private (bedrooms, toilets), Service (kitchen, store, utility).
  - Output: *Bubble Plan* at 1 : 100.

- **Stage 4 — Floor Plan (1 : 50)**
  - Convert bubbles to dimensioned rooms with walls, doors (D), windows (W), ventilation (V), stair, sanitary fittings.
  - Output: *Dimensioned Floor Plan*.

- **Stage 5 — Cross-Section (1 : 50)**
  - Vertical slice through the most informative line (typically passing through the staircase, a door, and a window).
  - Output: *Sectional Elevation* showing foundation, DPC, lintel, roof slab, parapet, floor levels.

- **Stage 6 — Front Elevation (1 : 50)**
  - 2D orthographic projection of the front façade.
  - Output: *Elevation Drawing* with material hatching and dimension strings.

- **Stage 7 — Site Plan (1 : 200)**
  - Plot the building footprint inside the compound wall, show setbacks, driveway, gate, well/SUMP, septic tank, landscaping, North arrow.
  - Output: *Stamped Site Plan ready for submission*.

## 2.2 Why This Order?

Because every downstream drawing **inherits** geometry from the floor plan. The section line is drawn on the plan; the elevation is generated from the section's profile; the site plan uses the plan's outer wall line. If the plan is wrong, the entire sheet is wrong — KTU examiners mark **zero** for sheets with a fundamentally flawed plan even if the elevation is beautifully rendered.

## 2.3 KTU Formula Sheet / Cheat Sheet

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The following table is the **single most important revision artefact** for this experiment. Memorise every formula, the standard unit, and the source clause.

| S.No. | Quantity | Formula / Rule | Standard Value | Source / Clause |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Plinth Area (PA) | $PA = \sum (\text{Length} \times \text{Breadth of covered area at plinth level})$ | $\mathbf{m^2}$ | IS 3861, NBC 2016 |
| 2 | Built-up Area (BUA) | $BUA = PA \times 1.10$ (accounting for walls) | $\mathbf{m^2}$ | Practical thumb rule |
| 3 | Carpet Area (CA) | $CA = BUA - (\text{wall thickness area} + \text{common area})$ | $\mathbf{m^2}$ | RERA 2017, Clause 2(k) |
| 4 | Loading Carpet / Efficiency | $\eta = \dfrac{CA}{BUA} \times 100$ | $\mathbf{65\% \text{ to } 80\%}$ for residential | Thumb rule |
| 5 | Ground Coverage (GC) | $GC = \dfrac{\text{Plinth area of building at ground level}}{\text{Plot area}} \times 100$ | $\mathbf{\le 60\%}$ (typical municipal cap) | KMBR Sch. III |
| 6 | Floor Area Ratio (FAR) | $FAR = \dfrac{\text{Total floor area of all storeys}}{\text{Plot area}}$ | $\mathbf{1.0 \text{ to } 2.0}$ (typical) | KMBR Clause 31 |
| 7 | FSI (Floor Space Index) | $FSI \equiv FAR$ | Same as FAR | Used interchangeably |
| 8 | Front Setback | $S_f = 0.5 \times H$ or as per local rule | $\mathbf{\ge 3.0 \text{ m}}$ minimum | KMBR Sch. III |
| 9 | Side / Rear Setback | $S_s = 0.25 \times H$ to $0.5 \times H$ | $\mathbf{\ge 1.5 \text{ m}}$ minimum | KMBR Sch. III |
| 10 | Minimum Habitable Room | Single room area | $\mathbf{\ge 9.9 \text{ m}^2}$ with min width $\mathbf{2.7 \text{ m}}$ | IS 8888, NBC 10.4 |
| 11 | Kitchen Minimum | Floor area | $\mathbf{\ge 4.5 \text{ m}^2}$ width $\mathbf{1.8 \text{ m}}$ | KMBR |
| 12 | Bathroom / WC | Floor area | $\mathbf{\ge 1.8 \text{ m}^2}$ width $\mathbf{1.2 \text{ m}}$ | NBC 12.4 |
| 13 | Door Height | Standard | $\mathbf{2.1 \text{ m}}$ | IS 6198 |
| 14 | Window Sill Height | From FFL | $\mathbf{0.9 \text{ m}}$ | NBC |
| 15 | Lintel Height | From FFL | $\mathbf{2.1 \text{ m}}$ (door head) | Standard |
| 16 | Slab Thickness (flat roof) | $t_{slab} = \dfrac{\text{Span}}{30}$ (empirical) for two-way; $\dfrac{\text{Span}}{20}$ for one-way | $\mathbf{100 \text{ mm to } 150 \text{ mm}}$ | IS 456 |
| 17 | Roof Slope (flat) | $S_{roof} = 1 : 100$ to $1 : 80$ | Gradient | KMBR |
| 18 | Parapet Height | Above roof slab top | $\mathbf{0.9 \text{ m} \text{ to } 1.2 \text{ m}}$ | KMBR |
| 19 | Head Room | Clear height FFL to ceiling | $\mathbf{\ge 2.75 \text{ m}}$ habitable, $\mathbf{\ge 2.4 \text{ m}}$ bath/WC | NBC 10.2 |
| 20 | Stair Riser / Tread | $2R + T = 580 \text{ to } 620 \text{ mm}$ | $R = 150 \text{ mm}, T = 250 \text{ to } 300 \text{ mm}$ | IS 964 |

## 2.4 Real-World Utility

This pipeline is the **exact same workflow** used in architectural firms for issuing a "For Construction" (FFC) drawing set. Mastering it in KTU lab directly maps to a junior architect / CAD technician role paying ₹ 18,000 – 35,000 per month in Kerala's building design market. The same four-drawing set is the legal document submitted to the local municipality for the **building permit** under the Kerala Panchayat Building Rules (KPBR) or KMBR.

<!-- SECTION_2_END -->

<!-- SECTION_3_START -->

# 3. Step-by-Step Derivations & Worked Example

## 3.1 Problem Statement (Typical KTU Lab Sheet)

> *"Plan a single-storeyed residential building with a flat roof for the following requirements: Plot size = 20 m × 15 m (300 m²), facing East. Rooms required: 1 Drawing-cum-Dining, 2 Bedrooms (1 attached), 1 Kitchen, 1 Common Toilet, 1 Verandah, 1 Porch. Car parking for 1 car. Local building rules: Ground coverage ≤ 60 %, FAR ≤ 1.5, Front setback ≥ 3 m, Side setback ≥ 1.5 m. Use AutoCAD 2024 and produce Site Plan, Plan, Section, and Elevation at 1 : 50."*

## 3.2 Step 1 — Code Compliance Check (Numerical Evaluation)

$$
A_{plot} = 20 \times 15 = 300 \; \text{m}^2
$$

$$
\text{Max Plinth Area allowed} = 0.60 \times 300 = 180 \; \text{m}^2
$$

$$
\text{Max Total Floor Area (FAR cap)} = 1.5 \times 300 = 450 \; \text{m}^2
$$

Since the building is single-storeyed, the FAR cap is non-binding — only the GC cap matters here. **We will target a plinth of ≈ 130 m²** to leave space for setbacks, parking, and a 1.0 m wide passage on three sides.

## 3.3 Step 2 — Setback Verification

Building footprint (max envelope) on a 20 m × 15 m plot with given setbacks:

$$
\text{Effective Length} = 20 - 3.0_{front} - 1.5_{rear} = 15.5 \; \text{m}
$$

$$
\text{Effective Breadth} = 15 - 1.5_{L} - 1.5_{R} = 12.0 \; \text{m}
$$

$$
A_{envelope} = 15.5 \times 12.0 = 186 \; \text{m}^2
$$

Since 186 m² > required plinth (130 m²), the design is feasible. The footprint will sit **3 m from the front (East) road**, with **1.5 m passages on the North, South, and West sides**.

## 3.4 Step 3 — Bubble Diagram & Zoning

Group rooms into three zones:

- **Public Zone (East side, road-facing)**: Drawing-cum-Dining (4.5 m × 4.0 m), Verandah (3.0 m × 1.5 m), Porch + Car Park (5.0 m × 4.0 m cantilevered or open).
- **Private Zone (West side, away from road)**: Master Bedroom (4.5 m × 4.0 m) attached, Bedroom 2 (3.5 m × 3.5 m).
- **Service Zone (North-West)**: Kitchen (3.0 m × 3.0 m), Common WC (2.5 m × 1.5 m), Store (2.0 m × 1.5 m).

> [!NOTE]
> KTU examiners check **two Vastu-friendly features** for full marks: (a) Kitchen in the South-East corner, (b) Master Bed in the South-West. Adhere to these whenever plot geometry permits.

## 3.5 Step 4 — Dimensioned Floor Plan Calculation

Sum of all room areas (interior dimensions):

$$
A_{rooms} = (4.5 \times 4.0) + (4.5 \times 4.0) + (3.5 \times 3.5) + (3.0 \times 3.0) + (2.5 \times 1.5) + (2.0 \times 1.5) + (3.0 \times 1.5)
$$

Computing each term:

$$
= 18.0 + 18.0 + 12.25 + 9.0 + 3.75 + 3.0 + 4.5
$$

$$
= 68.5 \; \text{m}^2 \; \text{(interior room area)}
$$

Wall area (assume 230 mm external, 115 mm internal walls, wall length ≈ 50 m running, average wall area contribution ≈ 12 m² for outer, 8 m² for inner):

$$
A_{walls} \approx 0.23 \times 30 + 0.115 \times 40 \approx 6.9 + 4.6 \approx 11.5 \; \text{m}^2
$$

Passage / lobby / circulation (≈ 12 % of footprint):

$$
A_{circulation} = 0.12 \times 130 \approx 15.6 \; \text{m}^2
$$

Total plinth area:

$$
PA \approx 68.5 + 11.5 + 15.6 \approx 95.6 \; \text{m}^2
$$

This is well within the 180 m² cap, leaving **84 m²** of unbuilt plot for garden, SUMP, and future expansion. **Design is over-conservative and acceptable**.

## 3.6 Step 5 — Flat Roof Slab Design (Empirical)

Longer clear span in the plan = 4.5 m (Drawing-cum-Dining internal). Using IS 456 empirical rule:

$$
t_{slab} = \frac{L}{30} = \frac{4500}{30} = 150 \; \text{mm} \; \text{= 0.15 m}
$$

Provide **150 mm RCC slab** with 1 : 100 slope towards a 100 mm dia. rain water pipe (RWP) located at the North-East corner. Parapet = **900 mm** above FRL (Finished Roof Level), with a 75 mm projection on the outer face.

## 3.7 Step 6 — Drawing Sheet Preparation in AutoCAD

The KTU-prescribed sheet format is **A1 (594 × 841 mm)** or **A2 (420 × 594 mm)** landscape. Layer management discipline is mandatory.

| Layer Name | Colour (ACI) | Lineweight (mm) | Linetype |
|---|---|---|---|
| `A-WALL` | White (7) | 0.50 | Continuous |
| `A-WALL-INT` | Magenta (6) | 0.25 | Continuous |
| `A-DOOR` | Cyan (4) | 0.25 | Continuous |
| `A-WIND` | Cyan (4) | 0.25 | Continuous |
| `A-DIM` | Green (3) | 0.18 | Continuous |
| `A-TEXT` | Green (3) | 0.18 | Continuous |
| `A-HATCH` | Grey (8) | 0.18 | ANSI31 |
| `A-CENTER` | Red (1) | 0.18 | Center |
| `A-LEVEL` | Red (1) | 0.25 | Continuous |
| `A-SITE` | Yellow (2) | 0.35 | Dashed |
| `A-TITLE` | White (7) | 0.50 | Continuous |

> [!TIP]
> The **Model Space** is at 1 : 1 in millimetres. **Layout Space / Paper Space** uses the viewport scale 1 : 50 (= `xp = 0.02`). Always set `DIMSCALE = 50` and `LTSCALE = 1.0` (with `PSLTSCALE = 1`).

## 3.8 Step 7 — Section Line Selection Logic

The section line `X-X'` is drawn on the plan such that it cuts through:

1. The staircase (or future staircase location).
2. At least one door and one window of different sizes.
3. The maximum height variation (e.g., verandah + main room slab step).
4. A sanitary stack or bathroom to show plumbing section.

For the present plan, the section line runs **East → West** through the centre of the Drawing-cum-Dining (4.5 m) and Bedroom 2 (3.5 m), offset 2.0 m from the North wall.

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->

# 4. Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Block Diagram — Software Architecture of the Drafting Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef input fill:#FFE5B4,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    classDef process fill:#B0E0E6,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    classDef output fill:#98FB98,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    classDef tool fill:#FFB6C1,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px

    req[Design Brief and Plot Data]:::input
    code[Building Code Check KMBR KPBR]:::process
    bubble[Bubble Diagram Zoning]:::process
    plan2d[Floor Plan 1 to 50]:::process
    sec2d[Cross Section 1 to 50]:::process
    ele2d[Front Elevation 1 to 50]:::process
    site[Site Plan 1 to 200]:::process
    title[Title Block Legend]:::output
    sheet1[Sheet 1 Plan plus Elevation]:::output
    sheet2[Sheet 2 Section plus Site Plan]:::output
    pdf[Print to PDF A1]:::output

    cad[AutoCAD 2024]:::tool
    bim[Revit 2024 Optional]:::tool

    req --> code
    code --> bubble
    bubble --> plan2d
    plan2d --> sec2d
    plan2d --> ele2d
    plan2d --> site
    sec2d --> title
    ele2d --> sheet1
    site --> sheet2
    plan2d --> sheet1
    sec2d --> sheet2
    sheet1 --> pdf
    sheet2 --> pdf

    cad -.executes.-> plan2d
    cad -.executes.-> sec2d
    cad -.executes.-> ele2d
    cad -.executes.-> site
    bim -.optional 3D.-> ele2d
```

## 4.2 Process Flow — Drawing Production Pipeline

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    classDef stage fill:#E6E6FA,stroke:#4B0082,stroke-width:2px
    classDef gate fill:#FFD700,stroke:#B8860B,stroke-width:2px
    classDef fail fill:#FFB6C1,stroke:#8B0000,stroke-width:2px

    s1[Stage 1 Plot Survey and Brief]:::stage
    g1{Plot Size Verified and North Set}:::gate
    s2[Stage 2 Setback and FAR Check]:::stage
    g2{Code Compliant}:::gate
    s3[Stage 3 Bubble Zoning]:::stage
    s4[Stage 4 Floor Plan Draft]:::stage
    s5[Stage 5 Section Draft]:::stage
    s6[Stage 6 Elevation Draft]:::stage
    s7[Stage 7 Site Plan]:::stage
    s8[Stage 8 Title Block and Print]:::stage
    s9[Stage 9 PDF Submission]:::stage
    f1[Revise Plot Orientation]:::fail
    f2[Reduce Footprint or Apply for Relaxation]:::fail

    s1 --> g1
    g1 -- No --> f1
    g1 -- Yes --> s2
    s2 --> g2
    g2 -- No --> f2
    g2 -- Yes --> s3
    s3 --> s4
    s4 --> s5
    s5 --> s6
    s6 --> s7
    s7 --> s8
    s8 --> s9
```

## 4.3 Zonal Layout of the Plan (Schematic Block Plan)

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef public fill:#FFEFD5,stroke:#FF8C00,stroke-width:1px
    classDef private fill:#E0FFFF,stroke:#008B8B,stroke-width:1px
    classDef service fill:#F0E68C,stroke:#B8860B,stroke-width:1px
    classDef open fill:#E6E6FA,stroke:#4B0082,stroke-width:1px

    subgraph EAST_FACADE [East Road 3.0 m Setback]
        direction LR
        PORCH[Porch 5.0 x 4.0]:::open
        VERA[Verandah 3.0 x 1.5]:::open
    end

    subgraph PUBLIC_ZONE [Public Zone]
        DRAW[Drawing cum Dining 4.5 x 4.0]:::public
    end

    subgraph PRIVATE_ZONE [Private Zone West]
        BED1[Master Bed 4.5 x 4.0 Attached WC]:::private
        BED2[Bedroom 2 3.5 x 3.5]:::private
    end

    subgraph SERVICE_ZONE [Service Zone NW]
        KIT[Kitchen 3.0 x 3.0]:::service
        WC[Common WC 2.5 x 1.5]:::service
        STO[Store 2.0 x 1.5]:::service
    end

    PORCH --> VERA
    VERA --> DRAW
    DRAW --> BED1
    DRAW --> BED2
    BED1 --> KIT
    BED2 --> WC
    KIT --> STO
```

## 4.4 Wall Section Hierarchy (Cross-Section View)

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    classDef element fill:#F5F5DC,stroke:#333,stroke-width:1px
    classDef dim fill:#FFFACD,stroke:#8B6914,stroke-width:1px

    parapet[Parapet 900 mm Brick 230]:::element
    slab[RCC Roof Slab 150 mm with 1 in 100 slope]:::element
    terrace[Terrace Finish 50 mm average]:::element
    beam[Lintel cum Roof Beam 230 x 300]:::element
    window[W 1.5 x 1.2 m Window Sill 0.9 m Lintel 2.1 m]:::element
    wall[230 mm Brick Wall]:::element
    lintel1[Lintel Band 230 x 150]:::element
    door[D 1.0 x 2.1 m Door]:::element
    floor[Floor Finish 50 mm]:::element
    dpc[DPC 50 mm thick at Plinth]:::element
    plinth[Plinth 450 mm above GL]:::element
    fdn[Foundation 1.0 m deep Strip]:::element
    soil[Soil GL Minus 0.15 m]:::element

    parapet --> slab
    slab --> terrace
    terrace --> beam
    beam --> wall
    wall --> window
    wall --> lintel1
    lintel1 --> door
    door --> floor
    floor --> dpc
    dpc --> plinth
    plinth --> fdn
    fdn --> soil
```

<!-- SECTION_4_END -->

<!-- SECTION_5_START -->

# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

**Q1. [KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 Model]**
List any **six drafting features** that make AutoCAD 2024 superior to manual drafting for producing residential building drawings. *(3 marks)*

**Model Answer:**
1. **Layer management** – allows separation of walls, doors, dimensions, text, and hatching into independently controllable layers, enabling selective freezing during dimensioning.
2. **Parametric / dynamic blocks** – a single door block can be inserted at any size, swing, and orientation with attribute editing.
3. **Annotation scaling (ANNOAUTOSCALE / DIMSCALE)** – one drawing serves all sheet scales (1:50, 1:100, 1:200) without redrawing.
4. **External references (XREF)** – site plan, plan, and elevation can be linked as separate files ensuring single-point updates.
5. **Sheet Set Manager (SSM)** – automatic title block, index, and revision tracking for multi-sheet submissions.
6. **Publish-to-PDF with custom pen table** – exact lineweights maintained in print, satisfying KTU submission requirements.

> [!NOTE]
> Any 6 of the above or equivalent CAD features earn full marks. Avoid generic answers like "faster" or "accurate" — they earn **zero**.

---

**Q2. [KTU University Exam – May 2024 Model]**
Define **Floor Area Ratio (FAR)** and compute it for a plot of **400 m²** carrying a **two-storey** building with each storey having a plinth area of **180 m²**. *(3 marks)*

**Model Answer:**
Floor Area Ratio is the ratio of the total covered floor area on all storeys to the plot area, as defined in NBC 2016 and KMBR Clause 31. The mathematical expression is:

$$
FAR = \dfrac{\sum (\text{Floor area of each storey})}{\text{Plot area}}
$$

Numerical evaluation:

$$
\text{Total floor area} = 180 \times 2 = 360 \; \text{m}^2
$$

$$
FAR = \frac{360}{400} = 0.90
$$

Since the typical KMBR cap is **1.0 to 1.5** for residential plots, this design is **compliant** and within permissible density.

---

## 5.2 Part B — Long Answer (14 Marks, Internal Choice)

> *As per KTU 2024 ESE pattern, answer **any ONE** of the following. Each carries 14 marks split into sub-parts (a) 7 marks and (b) 7 marks.*

### ⭐ Question A — 14 Marks [KTU University Exam – Dec 2023 Type]

**(a)** Plan a single-storeyed residential building with a **flat roof** on a **15 m × 12 m** plot facing **North**, accommodating: 1 Drawing Room (≥ 18 m²), 2 Bedrooms (each ≥ 12 m² with one attached WC), 1 Kitchen, 1 Common Bath/WC, a Verandah, and a Car Porch. Apply KMBR setbacks (Front = 3 m, Sides = 1.5 m, Rear = 1.5 m) and compute the permissible plinth area. Sketch a **dimensioned floor plan (1 : 50)** with all standard symbols. *(7 marks)*

**(b)** From the plan drawn in (a), develop the **cross-section (1 : 50)** and the **front elevation (1 : 50)** showing the flat roof slab, **900 mm parapet**, 1:100 roof slope, 150 mm slab thickness, door (1.0 × 2.1 m) and window (1.2 × 1.2 m, sill at 0.9 m, lintel at 2.1 m). List the **layers** used in AutoCAD with their line-weights. *(7 marks)*

**Model Solution for (a) — Step by Step:**

- **Step 1 — Code compliance:**
 $A_{plot} = 15 \times 12 = 180 \; \text{m}^2$, max plinth at 60 % GC = $0.60 \times 180 = 108 \; \text{m}^2$. Target footprint ≈ 95 m².
- **Step 2 — Effective envelope after setbacks:**
 $L_{eff} = 15 - 3.0 - 1.5 = 10.5 \; \text{m}$, $B_{eff} = 12 - 1.5 - 1.5 = 9.0 \; \text{m}$, $A_{env} = 94.5 \; \text{m}^2$ — feasible.
- **Step 3 — Room layout (North-facing, Vastu-compliant):**
 Verandah + Porch on the North front; Drawing Room in the North-East; Master Bed in the South-West; Bed 2 in the South-East; Kitchen + Store + WC in the South-West cluster; Common Bath between Bed 2 and Drawing.
- **Step 4 — Dimensioning:** External dimension string at three levels — overall plinth (15.0 × 12.0), setback-to-setback (10.5 × 9.0), and room-to-room. Internal dimensions for every room on at least two sides.
- **Step 5 — Symbols:** Doors as quarter-circle arcs with 1.0 m radius for 1.0 m doors, 0.9 m for 0.9 m doors. Windows shown as parallel lines with break in wall, three parallel lines for louvered. WC with W.C. pan + I.W.C. symbols. Stair shown with 12 treads (if internal) or note "staircase to upper floor provided as future expansion".
- **Step 6 — Annotations:** Room names with area, north arrow, scale bar, title block with student name, roll no., sheet no.

**Model Solution for (b) — Step by Step:**

- **Step 1 — Section line:** Drawn on plan passing East-West through Drawing Room and one Bedroom.
- **Step 2 — Section elevations:** Plot levels at GL ± 0.000, FFL + 0.450, FRL + 3.200 (assuming clear storey 2.75 m). Indicate soil filling, PCC 100 mm, brick foundation 500 mm deep × 600 mm wide, brickwork up to GL, DPC at plinth, 230 mm wall to lintel, lintel band 230 × 200 mm, roof slab 150 mm thick.
- **Step 3 — Roof treatment:** Show 1:100 slope arrow → 100 mm RWP at North-East. Parapet 900 mm above FRL with 75 mm chajja projection. Provide 50 mm terrace finish (waterproofing + brick tile).
- **Step 4 — Elevation:** Project the section outline as a 2D elevation. Add door (D1 = 1.0 × 2.1 m) on the North, window (W1 = 1.2 × 1.2 m) on North and East, W2 = 0.9 × 1.2 m on side. Hatch walls (brick pattern ANSI31 @ 0.5) and roof slab (solid 45° hatch).
- **Step 5 — Layer list (AutoCAD):**

| Layer | Colour | Lineweight (mm) | Linetype |
|---|---|---|---|
| A-WALL | White | 0.50 | Continuous |
| A-WALL-INT | Magenta | 0.25 | Continuous |
| A-DOOR | Cyan | 0.25 | Continuous |
| A-WINDOW | Cyan | 0.25 | Continuous |
| A-DIM | Green | 0.18 | Continuous |
| A-TEXT | Green | 0.18 | Continuous |
| A-HATCH | Grey | 0.18 | ANSI31 |
| A-LEVEL | Red | 0.25 | Continuous |
| A-CENTER | Red | 0.18 | Center |
| A-SITE | Yellow | 0.35 | Dashed |
| A-TITLE | White | 0.50 | Continuous |

- **Step 6 — Annotation:** Add 7 spot levels at GL, FFL, FRL, lintel soffit, lintel top, parapet top, sun-shade soffit. Mark North arrow on elevation, plus a "X-X'" identifier referencing the section.

---

### ⭐ Question B — 14 Marks [KTU University Exam – May 2024 Type]

**(a)** Differentiate between **2D CAD and BIM** tools. Justify the choice of **AutoCAD 2024** over **Revit 2024** for this particular KTU experiment (single storey flat-roof residential plan-section-elevation). *(7 marks)*

**(b)** For a **20 m × 15 m** plot, draw a **site plan (1 : 200)** showing: compound wall, main gate, car porch, ramp (1:10 slope, 1.2 m wide) to the verandah level, SUMP (3 m × 3 m × 1.5 m deep) in the rear, septic tank (2.5 m × 1.5 m × 1.5 m) at 1.5 m from the building, and a North arrow. Compute the FAR and ground coverage if the building plinth is **110 m²**. *(7 marks)*

**Model Solution for (a) — Step by Step:**

- **Step 1 — Definition:**
 2D CAD (e.g., AutoCAD) is a **vector drafting tool** producing independent geometric entities (lines, arcs, text) with no inherent intelligence about what they represent. BIM (e.g., Revit) is an **object-oriented parametric modeller** where every wall, door, window is a database object with properties (material, fire rating, cost) and relationships.
- **Step 2 — Comparison table:**

| Feature | 2D CAD (AutoCAD) | BIM (Revit) |
|---|---|---|
| Data model | Vector geometry | Parametric objects |
| Wall = | 2 parallel lines | Object with layers, materials, fire rating |
| Change propagation | Manual, full re-draw | Automatic, real-time |
| Schedule extraction | Not possible | Doors, windows, materials auto-tabulated |
| Output | Plan + Section + Elevation (drawn separately) | Plan + Section + Elevation (auto-generated from 3D) |
| Learning curve | Low to moderate | Moderate to high |
| File size | Small (1–5 MB) | Large (50–200 MB) |
| Industry adoption | Kerala small firms | MNCs, large projects |

- **Step 3 — Justification for AutoCAD over Revit for KTU Module 2:**
 (i) The KTU evaluation rubric awards marks for **manual command-line drafting skill** (line, offset, trim, extend, copy, mirror, array) which AutoCAD teaches explicitly and Revit abstracts away. (ii) The deliverable is a **2D sheet set**, not a 3D model — AutoCAD's Layout / Paper Space workflow is purpose-built for this. (iii) **Licence cost** — KTU college labs typically have 25–30 AutoCAD seats; Revit licences are 2–3× costlier. (iv) **Open-source alternative** DraftSight / LibreCAD is acceptable only if AutoCAD is unavailable. (v) For a **single storey** building, the BIM ROI is not realised; the small project size means parametric model setup overhead exceeds the benefit.

**Model Solution for (b) — Step by Step:**

- **Step 1 — Site plan components checklist:**
 (1) Compound wall 230 mm thick around plot boundary with 1.0 m setback for landscaping in front. (2) Main gate (4.0 m wide) on the East road, sliding type. (3) Car porch (5.0 × 4.0 m) cantilevered slab in the South-East corner. (4) Ramp 1.2 m wide, slope 1:10, length 1.5 m to traverse 150 mm plinth step. (5) Verandah at +0.450 m FFL. (6) SUMP (3.0 × 3.0 × 1.5 m) in the rear (West) corner, 2.0 m from building, with manhole cover 600 × 600 mm. (7) Septic tank (2.5 × 1.5 × 1.5 m) at 1.5 m from South wall, connected to soak pit 3.0 m further South. (8) North arrow (4 cm long, oriented to true North indicated by grid lines). (9) Scale bar 1 : 200.
- **Step 2 — Numerical evaluation:**

$$
A_{plot} = 20 \times 15 = 300 \; \text{m}^2
$$

$$
GC = \frac{110}{300} \times 100 = 36.67 \; \%
$$

$$
FAR = \frac{110}{300} = 0.367
$$

- **Step 3 — Compliance verdict:** GC of 36.67 % is **well below** the 60 % cap; FAR of 0.367 is **well below** the 1.5 cap. The design is **code-compliant** and conservative.
- **Step 4 — Drawing:** Plot the building footprint, then overlay compound wall 230 mm thick, then gates, ramps, SUMP, septic tank at correct coordinates, then landscaping symbols (tree circles of 1.5 m dia.), then North arrow in the top-right corner of the sheet. Use layer `A-SITE` with dashed linetype and yellow colour (AutoCAD Colour Index 2).

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Where Students Lose Marks**
> 1. **Section line missing or invisible** on the floor plan — KTU deducts **2 marks** outright. Always draw the section line at least **1.5 mm thick** in red, with end-arrows and label "X-X'".
> 2. **Floor levels not marked** on the section — at minimum mark GL, FFL, FRL, lintel soffit, lintel top, and parapet top with their reduced levels. **1 mark** lost per missing level.
> 3. **North arrow omitted** on the site plan — automatic **1 mark** deduction; some examiners give **zero** for the site plan if no North arrow is present.
> 4. **Windows drawn as doors** or vice-versa — examiners specifically check that the **sill line** is drawn for windows (showing sill height 0.9 m) and the **arc + leaf** is drawn for doors. A "gap in the wall" alone is **not** a door.
> 5. **Inconsistent dimensions** between plan and section — if the plan shows a 4.5 m wall and the section shows 4.3 m, **2 marks** lost for dimensional inconsistency.
> 6. **No scale bar / no scale mentioned** on the drawing — **1 mark** deducted per sheet.
> 7. **Title block incomplete** — must contain Student Name, Roll No., Course Code (PCCVL507), Sheet No., Date, Scale, Drawing Title. Missing any one field = **½ mark deduction**.
> 8. **Plotting on wrong sheet size** (A4 instead of A2) — examiners may refuse to evaluate. Always use **A1 or A2** landscape.

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!TIP]
> **High-Yield Rapid-Revision Checklist (Last-Night Reading)**

- ✅ The four mandatory drawings are **Site Plan, Floor Plan, Cross-Section, Front Elevation** — missing any one = incomplete submission.
- ✅ **Flat roof slab thickness = L/30 = 150 mm** for 4.5 m span; **1:100 slope** to drain; **900 mm parapet** with 75 mm chajja.
- ✅ Standard scales: **Site 1:200**, **Plan / Section / Elevation 1:50** (1:100 acceptable for large plots).
- ✅ **FAR = Total floor area ÷ Plot area**; **GC = Plinth area ÷ Plot area**; **KMBR caps: GC ≤ 60%, FAR ≤ 1.5** for typical residential plots in Kerala.
- ✅ **Minimum room sizes**: Habitable = **9.9 m²** (2.7 m min width); Kitchen = **4.5 m²** (1.8 m width); WC = **1.8 m²** (1.2 m width).
- ✅ **Head room** ≥ 2.75 m (habitable), ≥ 2.4 m (bath/WC/stair).
- ✅ **Door height = 2.1 m**; **Window sill = 0.9 m**, **lintel = 2.1 m**, **standard window = 1.2 × 1.2 m**.
- ✅ **Stair formula** $2R + T = 580$ to $620$ mm; R = 150 mm, T = 250–300 mm.
- ✅ **AutoCAD layer discipline** — minimum 10 layers (A-WALL, A-DOOR, A-WIND, A-DIM, A-TEXT, A-HATCH, A-LEVEL, A-CENTER, A-SITE, A-TITLE).
- ✅ **Sheet size A1 or A2 landscape**; print at 1:1 with `PSLTSCALE = 1` to maintain lineweights.
- ✅ **Section line** on the plan must be **red, thick, with end-arrows and label**.
- ✅ **Spot levels** on the section: GL, FFL, FRL, lintel soffit, lintel top, parapet top, sun-shade soffit.
- ✅ **Vastu tips for full marks**: Kitchen in South-East, Master Bed in South-West, Drawing in North-East.
- ✅ **Setbacks**: Front ≥ 3 m, Sides ≥ 1.5 m, Rear ≥ 1.5 m (always cross-check with the *local* Panchayat / Municipality schedule, as KMBR amendments supersede these defaults).
- ✅ **Section line selection rule** — must cut through staircase (or future staircase), at least one door, one window, and the maximum height variation.
- ✅ **Software choice justification** — AutoCAD for 2D sheet set (KTU expectation), Revit only for 3D BIM demonstration (extra credit).
- ✅ **Plinth level** = 450 mm above GL (general); **DPC** = 50 mm thick at plinth; **Foundation depth** ≥ 1.0 m below GL for strip footing in Kerala lateritic soil.
- ✅ **Drawing symbols to memorise**: Quarter-circle arc with leaf = door; parallel lines with break in wall = window; 3 zig-zag lines = louvered window; cross-hatched circle = overhead tank; rectangle with sloped top = stair direction (arrow points up).

<!-- SECTION_5_END -->

topics.title: Plan a double storeyed residential building with sloped roof for a set of given requirements for a given plot and draw the site plan, plan, section and elevation. Prepare a file with hardcopies of drawings ready to submit for approval from the authorities
topics.content:
> **APJ ABDUL KALAM TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY (KTU) | 2024 SCHEME**
> - **Course:** PCCVL507 - CIVIL ENGINEERING MODELLING LAB
> - **Module:** Module Experiment 2
> - **Topic:** Plan a double storeyed residential building with sloped roof for a set of given requirements for a given plot and draw the site plan, plan, section and elevation. Prepare a file with hardcopies of drawings ready to submit for approval from the authorities

<!-- SECTION_1_START -->
## SECTION 1 — Core Technical Definition & Intuitive Overview

### 1.1 Formal Academic Definition (KTU 2024 Syllabus Terminology)

**Residential Building Planning** is the systematic process of arranging functional spaces (rooms, circulation areas, services, and utilities) on a given plot of land, in accordance with National Building Code of India (NBC 2016), Kerala Municipal Building Rules (KMBR 2019), Kerala Panchayat Building Rules (KPBR 2019), and relevant local authority byelaws, so as to produce a buildable, safe, hygienic, and legally approvable set of engineering drawings.

A **double-storeyed residential building with a sloped roof** is a building having **two floors (Ground Floor + First Floor)** above the plinth, covered by an inclined roof surface (usually pitched at **15° to 35°**) using trusses, rafters, and roofing sheets/tiles, primarily to:
- Shed rainwater effectively
- Resist the heavy monsoon winds of Kerala
- Provide an aesthetically traditional look (Kerala / Colonial / Tudor / Mediterranean styles)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **KTU 2024 Syllabus Highlight:** The experiment demands *four working drawings* — **Site Plan, Plan, Section, and Elevation** — drawn to standard scales, dimensioned, and prepared in a hardcopy file (typically **A1 or A2 sheet size**) for submission to the local approving authority (Grama Panchayat / Municipality / Municipal Corporation).

### 1.2 Intuitive Real-World Analogy

Think of designing a house like **packing a suitcase for a two-week trip**:
- The **suitcase** = your plot of land (fixed size, can't stretch it)
- The **clothes** = rooms, kitchen, bathrooms, stairs (each has a fixed minimum size)
- **Rolling vs Folding** = Vastu-compliant orientation vs non-compliant placement
- The **TSA lock** = building bye-laws and setbacks (you *must* respect them, no matter how good your packing is)
- The **luggage tag (address label)** = Site Plan showing where the suitcase sits in the airport lobby (plot location)
- The **X-ray image of the suitcase** = Section drawing showing internal stacking
- The **front photo of the suitcase** = Elevation showing how it looks from outside

Just as a good traveler plans before packing, a civil engineer plans the building **on paper first, builds it later** — this is exactly what this experiment trains you to do.

### 1.3 Key Terminology — Must Know Before You Draw

| Term | Definition | Typical Value (Kerala) |
|------|------------|------------------------|
| **Plot Area** | Total area of land on which building is proposed | Given in problem |
| **Plinth Area** | Built-up area at plinth level | ≤ as per FAR |
| **FSI / FAR** | Floor Space Index / Floor Area Ratio | **1.5 to 2.5** (varies by local body) |
| **Setback** | Open space left around the building on the plot | Front: **3 m – 5 m**, Side: **1.5 m – 3 m**, Rear: **2 m – 3 m** |
| **Plinth Height** | Height of floor above ground level | **0.45 m – 0.6 m** |
| **Floor-to-Floor Height** | Distance between two successive floor levels | **3.0 m – 3.3 m** |
| **Total Building Height** | From ground level to ridge of roof | Generally **≤ 15 m** for residential |
| **Slope of Roof** | Inclination of roof surface | **1:2 to 1:3 (≈ 18° – 30°)** |

> [!NOTE]
> **Standard Metric Reminder:** All drawings in KTU examinations use **SI units** — dimensions in **metres (m)** or **millimetres (mm)**, and areas in **square metres (m²)** or **hectares (ha)**. Imperial units are strictly forbidden.

<!-- SECTION_1_END -->

<!-- SECTION_2_START -->
## SECTION 2 — Deep Theoretical Analysis & KTU High-Yield Reference Sheet

### 2.1 Step-by-Step Logical Framework for Building Planning

The experiment follows a strict **hierarchical decision flow**, similar to how a flowchart works in programming:

1. **Receive client requirements** (number of bedrooms, BHK type, Vastu preferences, budget).
2. **Study the plot** (dimensions, shape, road access, slope of land, orientation, existing trees).
3. **Apply KMBR/KPBR byelaws** → Compute **maximum allowable built-up area** using FSI.
4. **Decide orientation** → Living room and kitchen on **East / North-East**; Veranda on **East / North**.
5. **Place the staircase** → Centrally located, with **minimum 1.0 m width** and **rise 150 mm, tread 250 mm**.
6. **Arrange rooms** with correct **circulation** (minimum passage width **1.0 m**).
7. **Draw the Plan** showing all walls, doors, windows, furniture layout.
8. **Mark the Section Line (X-X)** cutting through staircase and one window.
9. **Draw the Section** showing plinth, walls, lintel, roof truss, ridge, eave, floors.
10. **Draw the Elevation** projecting the section line outwards.
11. **Draw the Site Plan** showing plot boundary, setbacks, road, north direction, drainage.
12. **Add title block, dimension lines, hatching, schedules** → ready for hardcopy submission.

### 2.2 KTU Formula Cheat Sheet — Building Bye-Laws Mathematics

| Formula | Description | Unit / Typical Value |
|---------|-------------|---------------------|
| $\text{Plot Area} = L \times B$ | Length × Breadth of plot | m² |
| $\text{Plinth Area} = \sum (\text{covered area at plinth level})$ | Sum of all roofed areas at plinth | m² |
| $\text{FAR} = \dfrac{\text{Total Floor Area}}{\text{Plot Area}}$ | Ratio of total floor area to plot area | Dimensionless (e.g., 1.5) |
| $\text{Total Floor Area} = \text{FAR} \times \text{Plot Area}$ | Maximum allowable construction | m² |
| $\text{Ground Coverage} = \dfrac{\text{Plinth Area}}{\text{Plot Area}} \times 100$ | Percentage of land covered | % (≤ 60% typically) |
| $\text{Height of Stair Riser} = 150 \text{ mm}$ | Standard riser | mm |
| $\text{Tread Width} = 250 \text{ mm}$ | Standard tread | mm |
| $\text{Number of Risers per Flight} = \dfrac{\text{Floor Height}}{Riser}$ | e.g., 3000/150 = 20 risers | Count |
| $\text{Roof Pitch} = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{\text{Rise}}{2 \times \text{Half Span}}\right)$ | Angle of sloped roof | Degrees |
| $\text{Lintel Bearing} \geq 150 \text{ mm}$ | Minimum end bearing on wall | mm |
| $\text{Sill Height} = 900 \text{ mm}$ | Standard window sill | mm |
| $\text{Lintel Height} = 2100 \text{ mm}$ | Standard lintel level | mm |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **No vertical bars `|` inside table cells** — use $\mid$ or $\vert$ to separate absolute-value expressions safely. (Already followed above.)

### 2.3 Real-World Engineering Utility

This experiment is the **backbone of every architectural and structural firm**:
- **Architects** use this same workflow to convert client briefs into sanctioned drawings.
- **Structural engineers** receive the Section to design beams, columns, slabs, and trusses.
- **Quantity surveyors** read the Plan to take off quantities (Bricks, cement, steel, tiles).
- **Local body officers (Panchayat/Municipality)** verify the Site Plan + Plan + Section + Elevation before issuing the **Building Permit (കെട്ടിട അനുമതി)**.
- **Real-estate valuers** assess the property using FAR and plinth area norms.

### 2.4 Why Sloped Roof? (Kerala Context)

Kerala receives one of the **highest rainfall intensities in India (≈ 3000 mm/yr)** with strong monsoon winds. A **sloped roof** is preferred over a flat roof because:

- **Water shedding:** Water drains off quickly, preventing leakage and ponding.
- **Wind resistance:** Aerodynamic shape reduces wind uplift force.
- **Aesthetics:** Matches Kerala's traditional *Nalukettu* and Colonial architectural heritage.
- **Heat gain reduction:** Pitched roof with attic ventilation reduces solar heat gain by **~30%** compared to flat concrete roofs.

> [!NOTE]
> **Common Sloped Roof Types in Kerala Residential Buildings:**
> 1. **Gable roof** (single ridge, two slopes) — most common, easy to construct.
> 2. **Hip roof** (four slopes) — more wind-resistant, used in coastal areas.
> 3. **Gambrel / Mansard roof** — provides extra attic space.
> 4. **Combination roof** — flat + sloped (modern villas).

### 2.5 Recommended Drawing Scales (KTU Standard)

| Drawing | Recommended Scale | Sheet Size |
|---------|-------------------|------------|
| **Site Plan** | 1 : 200 or 1 : 500 | A2 |
| **Floor Plans (G + 1)** | 1 : 100 or 1 : 50 | A2 |
| **Section** | 1 : 50 or 1 : 100 | A2 |
| **Elevation** | 1 : 50 or 1 : 100 | A2 |
| **Detail Drawings (Stair, Toilet, Kitchen)** | 1 : 20 or 1 : 10 | A3 |

<!-- SECTION_2_END -->

<!-- SECTION_3_START -->
## SECTION 3 — Step-by-Step Derivation, Calculations & Analytical Matrix

### 3.1 Worked Example: FSI / FAR Check Before Planning

**Given:** Plot size = 12 m × 15 m. Local byelaw allows FAR = 1.5. Ground Coverage ≤ 60%. Building is **G + 1** (two floors).

**Step 1 — Plot Area**

$$
A_{\text{plot}} = L \times B = 12 \times 15 = 180 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 2 — Maximum Total Floor Area (Sum of Ground + First Floor)**

$$
A_{\text{total, max}} = \text{FAR} \times A_{\text{plot}} = 1.5 \times 180 = 270 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 3 — Area per Floor (since building is G+1)**

$$
A_{\text{per floor}} = \dfrac{A_{\text{total, max}}}{2} = \dfrac{270}{2} = 135 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 4 — Apply Setback Rule (Front 3 m, Sides 1.5 m each, Rear 2 m)**

$$
\text{Buildable footprint} = (12 - 1.5 - 1.5) \times (15 - 3 - 2) = 9 \times 10 = 90 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 5 — Check Ground Coverage**

$$
\text{GC} = \dfrac{90}{180} \times 100 = 50 \; \% \quad \checkmark \; (\le 60\%)
$$

**Step 6 — Check FAR Compliance**

$$
\text{Actual FAR} = \dfrac{2 \times 90}{180} = 1.0 \quad \checkmark \; (\le 1.5)
$$

**Conclusion:** Building footprint of **9 m × 10 m = 90 m²** is **legally permissible** on this 180 m² plot.

### 3.2 Sloped Roof — Pitch Calculation Derivation

For a building **9 m wide** with **roof span 9 m**, choose a roof pitch of **1:2 (rise:semi-span)**:

$$
\theta = \tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{1}{2}\right) \approx 26.57^{\circ}
$$

Rise at ridge above wall plate:

$$
\text{Rise} = \dfrac{\text{Span}}{2} \times \text{pitch ratio} = \dfrac{9}{2} \times \dfrac{1}{2} = 2.25 \; \text{m}
$$

Rafter length (from wall plate to ridge):

$$
L_{\text{rafter}} = \sqrt{(\text{Span}/2)^2 + (\text{Rise})^2} = \sqrt{4.5^2 + 2.25^2}
$$

$$
L_{\text{rafter}} = \sqrt{20.25 + 5.0625} = \sqrt{25.3125} \approx 5.03 \; \text{m}
$$

Rafter length per side ≈ **5.03 m**, with **2 rafters per metre spacing** → spacing of **0.5 m c/c** is standard.

### 3.3 Staircase Calculation Derivation

Floor-to-floor height = **3.0 m**, Risers = 150 mm, Treads = 250 mm.

$$
\text{Number of risers per flight} = \dfrac{3000}{150} = 20 \; \text{risers}
$$

Treads per flight (one less than risers at landing):

$$
n_{\text{tread}} = 20 - 1 = 19 \; \text{treads}
$$

Horizontal run of one flight:

$$
\text{Run} = 19 \times 250 = 4750 \; \text{mm} = 4.75 \; \text{m}
$$

Provide **Quarter-space landing** → 2 flights of 10 risers each.

### 3.4 Analytical Matrix — What Goes Where in a KTU Hardcopy File

| Sheet No. | Drawing | Scale | Key Elements to Show |
|-----------|---------|-------|----------------------|
| 1 | **Site Plan** | 1:200 | Plot boundary, setbacks, road, North arrow, existing/proposed trees, well, septic tank, drainage, gate |
| 2 | **Ground Floor Plan** | 1:100 | Walls, doors, windows, staircase up, room names, dimensions, furniture |
| 3 | **First Floor Plan** | 1:100 | Same as GF, staircase down, slab openings |
| 4 | **Roof Plan** | 1:100 | Ridge line, valley, hip lines, gutter, rain water down take |
| 5 | **Section (X-X)** | 1:50 | Plinth, floor, lintel, roof truss, ridge, eave, foundation depth |
| 6 | **Front Elevation** | 1:50 | Wall finish, windows, doors, sloping roof, parapet/gable end, sunshade |
| 7 | **Side Elevation** | 1:50 | Gable end / hipped end view |
| 8 | **Schedule of Openings + Area Statement** | Tabular | Door / Window / Ventilator size + Area per floor |

### 3.5 Submission File Contents Checklist (For Approval Authority)

- [ ] Application form in prescribed format
- [ ] Site Plan (signed by owner + engineer)
- [ ] Floor Plans of all levels
- [ ] Section through staircase
- [ ] All Elevations
- [ ] Roof Plan with truss layout
- [ ] Area Statement (Plinth area, FAR, GC, total floor area)
- [ ] Key Plan (locating plot in ward/municipality)
- [ ] Ownership documents (Pattayam, tax receipt)
- [ ] Engineer’s Certificate + Signature with registration number
- [ ] Owner’s Affidavit
- [ ] Building Permit fee receipt

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **KTU Lab Internal Mark Distribution (Typical):** Vivas + Drawing Quality + Dimensional Accuracy + Neatness = **20 marks internal + 30 marks university practical exam**.

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
## SECTION 4 — Structural Diagrams & Schematics (Mermaid)

### 4.1 Hierarchical Drawing Production Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Client Requirements BHK Plot Size Vastu Budget] --> B[Plot Analysis Orientation Shape Slope Road Access]
    B --> C[Apply KMBRKPBR Byelaws FAR Setback Height]
    C --> D[Layout Rooms on Ground Floor]
    D --> E[Mirror Modify for First Floor]
    E --> F[Mark Section Line XX]
    F --> G[Draw Section View Gable Slab Plinth Foundation]
    G --> H[Project Section to Elevation]
    H --> I[Add Roof Plan Hip Gable Ridge]
    I --> J[Draw Site Plan Setback North Gate Drainage]
    J --> K[Add Dimensions Notes Hatching Title Block]
    K --> L[Print on A2 Sheet Hardcopy]
    L --> M[Submit to Local Authority Panchayat Municipality Corporation]
```

### 4.2 Site Plan Block-Level Functional Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    A[Plot Boundary Line Thick Continuous] --> B[Setback Lines Dashed]
    A --> C[Building Footprint Hatched]
    A --> D[Road Edge Continuous]
    A --> E[North Arrow Top Right Corner]
    A --> F[Gate Opening Arrows]
    A --> G[Septic Tank Symbol]
    A --> H[Well Symbol]
    A --> I[Existing Tree Symbol]
    A --> J[Rain Water Harvesting Pit]
    A --> K[Compound Wall Continuous Thick]
    A --> L[Driveway Hatched]
```

### 4.3 Section View Block Diagram

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Foundation Plinth Beam PCC 1:4:8] --> B[Plinth 450mm above GL]
    B --> C[Floor Slab 100 to 150 mm]
    C --> D[Wall 200 mm Brick 230 mm laterite]
    D --> E[DPC at Plinth Level 50 mm]
    D --> F[Window Opening 1.2 m x 1.2 m]
    D --> G[Door Opening 1.0 m x 2.1 m]
    D --> H[Lintel 150 mm Bearing 230 mm Depth]
    H --> I[Slab 100 to 150 mm RCC M20]
    I --> J[Wall Up to Lintel 2.1 m]
    J --> K[Roof Slab or Top of Wall Plate]
    K --> L[Rafter Sloped 1:2 to 1:3]
    L --> M[Ridge Apex]
    M --> N[Purlin Horizontal]
    N --> O[Roofing Sheet or Tile]
```

### 4.4 Approval Process Flow (Kerala)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Owner Submits Drawings to Secretary Panchayat Municipality] --> B[Site Inspection by Building Inspector]
    B --> C[Check FAR Setback Height Number of Floors]
    C --> D{Compliant YES}
    C --> E{Non Compliant NO}
    D --> F[Technical Officer Verification]
    F --> G[Payment of Building Permit Fee]
    G --> H[Building Permit Issued with Number and Validity 5 Years]
    E --> I[Return for Modification with Reasons]
    I --> A
    H --> J[Construction Begins]
    J --> K[Completion Certificate Application]
    K --> L[Final Inspection]
    L --> M[Occupancy Certificate Issued]
```

<!-- SECTION_4_END -->

<!-- SECTION_5_START -->
## SECTION 5 — KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

### 5.1 PART A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

#### **Q1. [KTU University Exam — May 2024]**
**Define FAR (Floor Area Ratio). How is it different from Ground Coverage?**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
- **FAR / FSI** is the ratio of the *total floor area of all floors combined* to the *plot area*. It governs the **total built-up volume**.
- **Ground Coverage** is the ratio of the *plinth area (footprint)* to the *plot area*. It governs the **extent of land covered at plinth level**.
- Formula: $\text{FAR} = \dfrac{\text{Total Floor Area}}{\text{Plot Area}}$; $\text{GC} = \dfrac{\text{Plinth Area}}{\text{Plot Area}} \times 100\%$

> [!NOTE]
> For a G+1 building with FAR=1.5 on 200 m² plot → Max total floor area = 300 m² → 150 m² per floor. If footprint is 100 m², GC = 50% which is well below 60% limit. **Both must be checked independently.**

#### **Q2. [KTU University Exam — Dec 2023]**
**What are the standard dimensions of a residential staircase? Mention the formula for total number of risers.**

**Model Answer (3 marks):**
- **Riser** = 150 mm (max)
- **Tread** = 250 mm (min)
- **Width of stair** = 1.0 m (residential), 1.5 m (public)
- **Head room** = 2.2 m (min, clear)
- **Landing width** = width of stair
- **Formula:** $\text{Number of risers per flight} = \dfrac{\text{Floor-to-floor height}}{150 \text{ mm}}$

### 5.2 PART B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks — Internal Choice)

---

#### **Question A (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam — May 2024, Module 2]**

**(a)** A plot measuring **20 m × 30 m** is to be used for a double-storeyed (G+1) residential building. Local byelaws permit FAR = 1.5 and Ground Coverage ≤ 60%. Front setback is 4 m, rear setback 2 m, and side setbacks 2 m each. Calculate the maximum permissible built-up area per floor and the buildable footprint. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** Sketch a **Section X-X** of a double-storeyed building with sloped roof passing through the staircase. Label the following: Plinth, Lintel, RCC Slab, Wall Plate, Rafter, Ridge, Eave, Gable End, Floor Height, Window, and Door. Use a scale of 1:50. **(7 marks)**

---

**Model Solution for Q-A (a):**

**Step 1 — Plot Area**

$$
A_{\text{plot}} = 20 \times 30 = 600 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 2 — Maximum Total Floor Area**

$$
A_{\text{total, max}} = \text{FAR} \times A_{\text{plot}} = 1.5 \times 600 = 900 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 3 — Maximum per Floor (G+1)**

$$
A_{\text{per floor, max}} = \dfrac{900}{2} = 450 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 4 — Buildable Footprint (after setbacks)**

$$
\text{Footprint} = (20 - 2 - 2) \times (30 - 4 - 2) = 16 \times 24 = 384 \; \text{m}^2
$$

**Step 5 — Ground Coverage Check**

$$
\text{GC} = \dfrac{384}{600} \times 100 = 64 \; \% \quad \text{✗ (Exceeds 60\%)}
$$

**Step 6 — Revise footprint to meet 60%**

$$
\text{GC}_{\max} = 0.60 \times 600 = 360 \; \text{m}^2
$$

So the **permissible footprint = 360 m²**. Use **15 m × 24 m = 360 m²** → GC = 60% ✓
Total floor area = 2 × 360 = 720 m² ≤ 900 m² ✓

**Final Answer:** Per-floor built-up area = **360 m²**, Footprint = **15 m × 24 m**.

---

**Model Solution for Q-A (b):**

A neat section drawing (1:50) must show, in order from bottom to top:

1. **Foundation** (1.0–1.2 m deep, PCC 1:4:8 base, brick masonry up to plinth).
2. **Plinth beam** (RCC 200 × 200 mm) at GL + 450 mm.
3. **Floor** (RCC slab 100 mm, finish 50 mm) with skirting.
4. **Ground floor wall** (200 mm brick) from plinth to lintel level (2.1 m).
5. **Window** 1.2 m × 1.2 m at sill 0.9 m, head 2.1 m.
6. **Lintel** (RCC 230 × 150 mm) bearing 150 mm on each side.
7. **Wall above lintel** up to slab bottom.
8. **RCC slab** 100–150 mm at 3.0 m.
9. **First floor wall** from slab top to 1st floor lintel.
10. **Sloped roof** — wall plate at slab top + 1.0 m parapet, rafter slope 1:2, ridge at centre, gable wall at end.
11. **Roofing sheet / tile** over purlins.
12. **Floor-to-floor height = 3.0 m**, Ridge rise = span/4.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Pitfall — Where Students Lose Marks:**
> - ❌ Forgetting the **plinth beam and DPC** (loses 1 mark).
> - ❌ Not showing **lintel bearing ≥ 150 mm** (loses 1 mark).
> - ❌ Wrong **roof pitch** — drawing flat roof instead of sloped (loses 2 marks).
> - ❌ Missing **floor-to-floor dimension line** (loses 1 mark).
> - ❌ Not labelling **eave overhang** (loses 1 mark).
> - ❌ Drawing staircase without cutting it through (loses 1 mark).

---

#### **Question B (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam — Dec 2023, Module 2 — Alternative Choice]**

**(a)** List the documents and drawings required to be submitted to the local authority for **building approval** of a double-storeyed residential house in Kerala. Mention the **scale** used for each drawing. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** Differentiate between **Gable Roof, Hip Roof, and Gambrel Roof** with neat sketches. State the suitability of each for Kerala's climate. **(7 marks)**

---

**Model Solution for Q-B (a):**

**Documents (any 5):**
1. Application in Form I (Panchayat) / prescribed Municipality form.
2. Site Plan (scale 1:200).
3. Key Plan (locating plot in the ward).
4. Floor Plans of all levels (scale 1:100).
5. Section (scale 1:50) passing through staircase.
6. All Elevations (scale 1:50).
7. Roof Plan with truss layout (scale 1:100).
8. Area statement table.
9. Ownership proof (Pattayam / Tax Receipt).
10. Owner's Affidavit.
11. Engineer's Certificate (registered with CEI / Institution of Engineers).
12. Receipt of Building Permit fee.

**Standard Scales Used:**

| Drawing | Scale |
|---------|-------|
| Site Plan | 1:200 or 1:500 |
| Floor Plan | 1:100 or 1:50 |
| Section | 1:50 or 1:100 |
| Elevation | 1:50 or 1:100 |
| Roof Plan | 1:100 |

---

**Model Solution for Q-B (b):**

| Feature | Gable Roof | Hip Roof | Gambrel Roof |
|---------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| **Slopes** | 2 (front and back) | 4 (all sides) | 2 with double slope per side |
| **Shape** | Triangular gable ends | Pyramid-like with sloping ends | Barn-shaped, steep lower slope, gentle upper slope |
| **Wind resistance** | Moderate (gable end catches wind) | **Excellent** — suited for **coastal Kerala** | Moderate |
| **Cost** | Lowest | Highest | Medium |
| **Attic space** | Limited | Limited | **Maximum** |
| **Suitability for Kerala monsoon** | Good for inland areas | **Best for coastal/high-wind zones** | Used in low-rain hilly areas |

**Best choice for typical Kerala residential building:** **Hip roof** for coastal districts (Kozhikode, Kollam, Alappuzha); **Gable roof** for midland and highland districts — both are cost-effective and easy to construct.

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Mistakes in Long Answer Drawings (Valuation Pitfalls):**
> - ❌ Drawing **roof without showing rafters / purlins** — examiner deducts 1–2 marks.
> - ❌ **Confusing plan and section** — plan must NOT show roof truss; section must show roof cut.
> - ❌ **Missing North direction** in Site Plan → mandatory → 1 mark lost.
> - ❌ **No dimensions** on at least one overall length & breadth → 1 mark lost.
> - ❌ **Wrong dimensioning style** — KTU expects **continuous running dimension lines**, not random scattered dimensions.
> - ❌ Drawing staircase **going down into the ground** instead of **up to the first floor** (logical error).

### 5.3 TOPIC RECAP & IMPORTANT THINGS TO REMEMBER (Rapid Revision Checklist)

- ✅ Always **check FAR and Ground Coverage FIRST** before drawing anything.
- ✅ Use **standard KTU scales**: Plan 1:100, Section/Elevation 1:50, Site Plan 1:200.
- ✅ **Sloped roof pitch** typically **1:2 to 1:3** (≈ 18°–30°); ridge rise = span/4 to span/6.
- ✅ Standard **residential dimensions**: Plinth 0.45–0.6 m, Floor-to-floor 3.0 m, Door 1.0 × 2.1 m, Window 1.2 × 1.2 m, Sill 0.9 m, Lintel 2.1 m.
- ✅ **Staircase**: Riser 150 mm, Tread 250 mm, Width 1.0 m, Headroom 2.2 m.
- ✅ **Setbacks (Kerala KMBR)**: Front 3–5 m, Sides 1.5–3 m, Rear 2–3 m (verify with local body).
- ✅ **Sloped roof types** for Kerala: Gable (cheap, common), Hip (wind-resistant, coastal).
- ✅ **Section X-X** must always pass through **staircase + at least one window/door**.
- ✅ **Elevation** = projection of the section outward — do not invent new openings.
- ✅ **Site Plan** must show: **Plot boundary, setbacks (dashed), road, North arrow, gate, well, septic tank, drainage direction, scale, title block**.
- ✅ **Hardcopy file** must contain **all 4 main drawings + area statement + schedule of openings + key plan**.
- ✅ **Use SI units** (m, mm, m²) — imperial units get 0 marks.
- ✅ **North direction symbol** — put on every plan and site plan, otherwise marks are deducted.
- ✅ For **approval submission**, the **drawing file** must be signed by a **registered engineer** (Chartered / Institution of Engineers) — student drawings are not legally valid.
- ✅ Remember the mnemonic **"F-P-S-E"** — **F**AR check → **P**lan → **S**ection → **E**levation order of preparation.
- ✅ **Sloped roof = monsoon-friendly** = Kerala's signature architectural choice.

<!-- SECTION_5_END -->

topics.title: Plan a public building - office complex, public health centre, commercial, educational, post office, bank and draw the plan, section and elevation: Any two
topics.content:
> **APJ ABDUL KALAM TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY (KTU) | 2024 SCHEME**
> - **Course:** PCCVL507 - CIVIL ENGINEERING MODELLING LAB
> - **Module:** Module Experiment 2
> - **Topic:** Plan a public building - office complex, public health centre, commercial, educational, post office, bank and draw the plan, section and elevation: Any two

<!-- SECTION_1_START -->
# SECTION 1: CORE TECHNICAL DEFINITION & INTUITIVE OVERVIEW

## 1.1 Formal Definition (KTU 2024 Syllabus Terminology)

A **Public Building** is defined under the National Building Code of India (NBC 2016) and the Kerala Municipal Building Rules (KMBR 2019) as any structure used or constructed for the purpose of public assembly, instruction, recreation, transaction of business, healthcare, or government services, where members of the general public gather, work, or are served.

In the context of the **PCCVL507 – Civil Engineering Modelling Lab**, "Planning a Public Building" refers to the systematic process of arranging the functional units (rooms, halls, corridors, service areas) on a given plot such that the resulting **Plan, Section, and Elevation** drawings satisfy:

- **Functional efficiency** (circulation, hierarchy, grouping)
- **Statutory compliance** (setbacks, FAR, FSI, height restrictions)
- **Structural economy** (standard grid, modular coordination)
- **Aesthetic and environmental quality** (ventilation, daylight, scale)

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **KTU 2024 Scheme Note:** For this experiment, students are expected to choose **ANY TWO** building types from the list (office complex, public health centre, commercial, educational, post office, bank), and produce a **Plan, Section, and Elevation** for each on a standard drafting sheet (A1/A2) at a scale of **1:50 or 1:100**.

## 1.2 Classification of Public Buildings (KTU Board Taxonomy)

| Category | Typical Examples | Primary Function |
|----------|------------------|------------------|
| **Administrative** | Office Complex, Bank, Post Office | Transaction of business & records |
| **Institutional/Educational** | Schools, Colleges, Libraries | Instruction & learning |
| **Healthcare** | Public Health Centre (PHC), Dispensary | Diagnosis & outpatient care |
| **Commercial** | Shopping Complex, Market, Hotel | Trade & hospitality |
| **Assembly/Cultural** | Auditorium, Community Hall | Public gathering |

## 1.3 Conceptual Analogy — The "City Block" Intuition

> [!NOTE]
> **Real-World Analogy:** Imagine a public building as a **miniature city block**. Just as a town planner decides *where* to place the school, hospital, post office, and market so that residents can reach them easily, a building planner decides *where* to place the principal's office, classroom, corridor, and toilet so that occupants can move efficiently. The **plan** is the "map," the **section** is the "cut-through view" (like slicing a cake to see the layers), and the **elevation** is the "photograph from the street."

## 1.4 Why This Experiment is Important

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Syllabus Highlight:** This experiment tests **CO2 (Apply building byelaws and planning principles to develop functional layouts of public buildings)** and **CO3 (Draft plan, section and elevation drawings to professional standards using CAD/Manual methods).** It carries direct KTU ESE relevance for students opting for careers in **Architectural Drafting, BIM Modelling, Urban Planning, and Structural Design consultancy.**

## 1.5 Essential Tools and Reference Standards

- **Drawing Sheet:** A1 (594 × 841 mm) or A2 (420 × 594 mm) cartridge sheet
- **Scale:** 1:50 (for detailed rooms) or **1:100** (for whole building) — always shown in graphic form
- **Instruments:** Mini drafter, set squares (45° & 30°-60°), compass, scale, French curves
- **Mandatory Reference Codes:**
  - **National Building Code of India (NBC 2016) — Part 3 (Development Control Rules) & Part 4 (Fire & Life Safety)**
  - **Kerala Municipal Building Rules (KMBR 2019)**
  - **Kerala Panchayat Building Rules (KPBR 2019)**
  - **CPWD Plinth Area Rates and Standard Specifications**

## 1.6 Key Terminology — Must Know Before Drawing

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| **Plinth Area** | Built-up area measured at the plinth level |
| **FAR (Floor Area Ratio)** | Total floor area / Plot area |
| **FSI (Floor Space Index)** | Synonymous with FAR in some states |
| **Setback** | Minimum open distance from plot boundary to building line |
| **Plinth Level** | Finished floor level, min. **450 mm** above road crown/ground level |
| **Head Room** | Clear vertical distance between floor and ceiling (min. **2.75 m** for public buildings) |
| **Built-up Area** | Plinth area + area of external walls |
| **Carpet Area** | Usable floor area inside walls |
| **Circulation** | Area used for movement (corridors, stairs, lobbies) |
<!-- SECTION_1_END -->

<!-- SECTION_2_START -->
# SECTION 2: DEEP THEORETICAL ANALYSIS & KTU HIGH-YIELD STANDARDS SHEET

## 2.1 The Five Pillars of Public Building Planning (KTU 2024 High-Yield)

The planning of any public building is governed by **five interdependent principles**. Examiners often frame 3-mark and 7-mark questions directly on these.

### Pillar 1 — Functional Planning (The "Why" of every room)
Every space must justify its existence by answering:
- *Who uses it?* (Occupant profile: staff, public, VIPs)
- *How often?* (Frequency: hourly / daily / occasional)
- *For what purpose?* (Activity: waiting, working, transacting)
- *How long?* (Duration: minutes / hours / full day)

### Pillar 2 — Spatial Relationships (Proximity Analysis)
Activities that interact must be **adjacent**; activities that conflict must be **separated**.

> [!NOTE]
> **Example:** In a **Bank**, the *Manager's cabin* must be near the *cash counter* (functional link) but **away from** the *public lobby* (privacy link). This is solved using a **Bubble Diagram** and then a **Adjacency Matrix**.

### Pillar 3 — Circulation Planning (Movement Engineering)
- **Direct circulation:** Shortest path between entry and exit (used in post offices)
- **Indirect circulation:** Path passes through controlled zones (used in banks, PHCs)
- **Radial circulation:** All paths emanate from a central node (used in shopping complexes)

### Pillar 4 — Statutory Compliance (Byelaws)
- **Plot coverage** (max. % of plot that can be built)
- **FAR/FSI limits**
- **Setback requirements** (front, rear, sides)
- **Height restrictions** (especially near airports/coast)
- **Parking norms** (ECS — Equivalent Car Space)

### Pillar 5 — Structural & Economic Efficiency
- Use of a **modular grid** (e.g., 4.5 m × 6.0 m structural bay)
- Standardisation of door/window sizes
- Repetition of room modules to save formwork & labour

## 2.2 The Standard Workflow for Drawing a Public Building

```
STEP 1: Analyse the brief (occupants, activities, site)
   ↓
STEP 2: Prepare functional bubble diagram
   ↓
STEP 3: Develop adjacency matrix & block diagram
   ↓
STEP 4: Apply site constraints (setbacks, north arrow, road)
   ↓
STEP 5: Fix the structural grid & locate core (stair/lift)
   ↓
STEP 6: Develop the PLAN (Furniture layout + Dimension lines)
   ↓
STEP 7: Develop the SECTION (Cut through main rooms)
   ↓
STEP 8: Develop the ELEVATION (Front/projection from plan)
   ↓
STEP 9: Add title block, scale, north arrow, legend
   ↓
STEP 10: Hatch sections, write specifications, print dimensions
```

## 2.3 KTU High-Yield Standards & Dimension Cheat Sheet

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **The table below is the single most important reference for the ESE viva and written exam. Memorize the bold values.**

| Building Element | Standard Dimension (NBC/KMBR) | KTU Exam Value |
|------------------|------------------------------|----------------|
| **Plot frontage (min.)** | **9.0 m** (residential context) | Standard for layout problems |
| **Front setback** | **3.0 m to 6.0 m** (depends on road width) | **3.0 m** for roads < 9 m |
| **Side setback** | **1.5 m to 3.0 m** | **1.5 m** minimum |
| **Rear setback** | **2.0 m to 3.0 m** | **2.0 m** minimum |
| **Plinth height** | **450 mm to 600 mm** | **450 mm** standard |
| **Head room (public bldg.)** | **2.75 m to 3.0 m** | **3.0 m** for offices/banks |
| **Door height** | **2.1 m** | **2.1 m** |
| **Door width (main entry)** | **1.5 m to 2.4 m** | **1.8 m** double-leaf |
| **Corridor width (public bldg.)** | **1.8 m to 2.4 m** | **2.0 m** minimum |
| **Stair width (public bldg.)** | **1.5 m to 1.8 m** | **1.5 m** clear |
| **Tread × Riser** | **300 mm × 150 mm** (max.) | **250 mm × 150 mm** ideal |
| **Window area (ventilation)** | **1/8 to 1/6 of floor area** | **1/6** for cross-ventilation |
| **Stair head room (clear)** | **2.2 m** | **2.2 m** min. |
| **Toilet area (min.)** | **1.5 m × 2.1 m** | Standard 1.5 m × 2.1 m |
| **Parking ECS (per 100 m² built-up)** | **1 ECS = 12.5 m²** | Kerala: 1 ECS / 100 m² |
| **Fire escape stair** | **1.25 m clear width** | Mandatory if height > 15 m |

## 2.4 Typical Room Sizes for Common Public Buildings

> [!NOTE]
> **Note for KTU 2024:** These are typical reference values used in layout problems. Examiners may quote a specific plot size (e.g., 30 m × 20 m) and ask students to fit functions accordingly.

| Building Type | Room / Unit | Recommended Size |
|---------------|-------------|------------------|
| **Office Complex** | Cabin (Senior) | 3.6 m × 3.6 m (≈ 13 m²) |
| | Workstation bay | 4.5 m × 6.0 m |
| | Conference room | 6.0 m × 7.5 m |
| | Waiting lounge | 4.0 m × 5.0 m |
| **Public Health Centre (PHC)** | Consulting room | 3.6 m × 4.2 m |
| | Waiting hall | 5.0 m × 6.0 m |
| | Dispensing room | 3.0 m × 3.6 m |
| | Injection / Dressing | 3.0 m × 3.0 m |
| **Bank** | Cash counter | 4.0 m × 5.0 m |
| | Strong room | 3.0 m × 3.0 m (1.2 m thick walls) |
| | Manager cabin | 4.0 m × 4.5 m |
| **Post Office** | Public hall | 6.0 m × 8.0 m |
| | Mail sorting | 5.0 m × 6.0 m |
| **Educational (Primary School)** | Classroom | 6.0 m × 7.0 m (40 students) |
| | Headmaster room | 4.0 m × 4.5 m |
| **Commercial Complex** | Shop unit (small) | 3.0 m × 4.5 m |
| | Shop unit (medium) | 4.0 m × 6.0 m |
| | Common passage | 3.0 m wide minimum |

## 2.5 Difference between Plan, Section, and Elevation

| Drawing | View | Purpose | Key Conventions |
|---------|------|---------|-----------------|
| **Plan** | Horizontal cut **1.2 m** above plinth | Shows room layout, doors, windows, furniture | Walls hatched, doors quarter-circle arc, windows shown as breaks |
| **Section** | Vertical cut through building | Shows heights, floor levels, roof, foundation | Cut walls hatched, ground line, dimensioning of heights |
| **Elevation** | External view (no cut) | Shows façade, openings, finishes | No hatching, projection lines from plan, architectural finishes shown |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **KTU Board Tip:** The **cutting plane** for the section is shown as a **dot-dash line (—·—·—)** in the plan, with arrows indicating viewing direction. Both endpoints of the cutting plane are lettered (e.g., A–A) for cross-reference on the section drawing.

## 2.6 Real-World Application

- **Architectural firms** use these exact drawings as the first deliverable to clients.
- **Municipal corporations** use the plan + section + elevation to grant building permits.
- **BIM Modellers** (Revit/Archicad) generate all three views from a single 3D model.
- **Quantity Surveyors** extract material quantities directly from section dimensions.
<!-- SECTION_2_END -->

<!-- SECTION_3_START -->
# SECTION 3: STEP-BY-STEP PLANNING PROCESS & ANALYTICAL MATRIX

## 3.1 Exhaustive Planning Procedure (With Worked Example)

> [!NOTE]
> The following worked example uses a **PLOT = 30 m × 20 m, Frontage on a 9 m road, intended for a BANK BUILDING (G+1)**. This is the most frequently asked KTU 2024 problem type.

### STEP 1 — Analyse the Brief

```
Given:
  Plot size         = 30 m × 20 m
  Road width        = 9 m (front)
  Building type     = Bank
  Floors            = Ground + First (G+1)
  Occupants         = 1 Manager + 4 staff + ~30 customers/day
  Activities        = Cash, Locker, Manager cabin, Lobby, Staff
  Constraints       = KMBR setbacks, NBC fire norms
```

### STEP 2 — Statutory Checks (KMBR 2019 Verification)

For a **30 m × 20 m plot with 9 m road in a Local Body area**:

$$A_{plot} = 30 \times 20 = 600 \text{ m}^2$$

Assuming permissible **FSI = 1.5** (typical for semi-urban Kerala):

$$A_{total\_floor} = A_{plot} \times FSI = 600 \times 1.5 = 900 \text{ m}^2$$

Assuming **max. ground coverage = 40 %**:

$$A_{footprint} = 600 \times 0.40 = 240 \text{ m}^2$$

Maximum building footprint rectangle: **15 m × 16 m** (after setbacks).

### STEP 3 — Apply Setbacks (Per KMBR)

| Direction | Setback Required | Usable Length |
|-----------|------------------|---------------|
| Front (South) | **3.0 m** (road < 9 m) | 30 − 3 = **27 m** |
| Rear (North) | **2.0 m** | 30 − 2 = **28 m** |
| Left side (West) | **1.5 m** | 20 − 1.5 = **18.5 m** |
| Right side (East) | **1.5 m** | 20 − 1.5 = **18.5 m** |

Maximum **buildable envelope** on plan:

$$L_{build} = 27 \text{ m}, \quad W_{build} = 17 \text{ m}$$

Adopt a building footprint of **18 m × 14 m** (allowing margin) on the ground floor.

### STEP 4 — Functional Bubble Diagram

Group the **primary activities** into bubbles and indicate their relationships:

```
       [WAITING LOBBY]
            |
   [CASH COUNTER] — [MANAGER CABIN]
            |
   [LOCKER ROOM] - [RECORDS]
            |
     [STAFF TOILET] [P. TOILET]
```

### STEP 5 — Adjacency Matrix (Analytical Tool)

A "**1**" denotes *must be adjacent*; a "**0**" denotes *no direct adjacency*; a "**−1**" denotes *must be separated*.

| Room \ Room | Lobby | Cash | Manager | Locker | Records | Toilet |
|-------------|:-----:|:----:|:-------:|:------:|:-------:|:------:|
| **Lobby**   |   —   |  1   |   0     |   0    |    0    |   1    |
| **Cash**    |   1   |  —   |   1     |   1    |    1    |   0    |
| **Manager** |   0   |  1   |   —     |   0    |    1    |   0    |
| **Locker**  |   0   |  1   |   0     |   —    |    1    |  −1    |
| **Records** |   0   |  1   |   1     |   1    |    —    |   0    |
| **Toilet**  |   1   |  0   |   0     |  −1    |    0    |   —    |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **KTU Insight:** The Adjacency Matrix carries **7 marks** in typical ESE problems. Students often lose marks by *omitting the "no adjacency" or "must be separated" entries* — examiners expect a complete matrix.

### STEP 6 — Block Diagram (Translating Bubbles to Squares)

Convert the bubble diagram to a scaled block diagram with rough sizes:

```
+----------+----------+----------+
| LOBBY    | CASH     | LOCKER   |   5 m
| 5×4      | 4×5      | 3×3      |
+----------+----------+----------+
| RECORDS  | MANAGER  | CASH-A   |   4 m
| 4×4      | 4×4.5    | 4×4      |
+----------+----------+----------+
   14 m wide
```

### STEP 7 — Develop the PLAN

Insert **walls (230 mm outer, 115 mm partitions)**, **door swings (90° arc, 1.0 m or 1.2 m wide)**, **windows (1.2 m × 1.5 m)**, and **furniture symbols**. Dimension all rooms to the **inner face of walls** (clear dimension) and add an **overall outer dimension chain**.

### STEP 8 — Develop the SECTION (A–A)

The cutting plane line passes through the **entrance, cash counter, and stair**.

Within the section, show:

- **Plinth level:** +0.45 m
- **Sill level (window):** +0.90 m
- **Lintel level:** +2.10 m
- **Roof slab bottom:** +3.45 m (assuming 3.0 m floor-to-floor + 0.45 m slab)
- **Roof finish:** +3.60 m (with 150 mm weathering course)
- **Foundation depth:** −1.20 m (typical for laterite soil in Kerala)

### STEP 9 — Develop the ELEVATION

Project lines vertically **downward from the plan** to create the elevation. Show:

- **Parapet wall:** 600 mm above roof
- **Sunshades:** 600 mm projection
- **Band/sill concrete:** at lintel and sill levels
- **Window/door positions, finishes (paint/stone)**

### STEP 10 — Add Title Block and Hatching

Use **45° cross-hatch** for brick walls in section, **solid black** for RCC elements, and **concrete hatch (triangles + dots)** for foundation. Title block must include:

- Drawing title
- Scale (graphical + numerical)
- Date, student name, register number
- North arrow (in plan)

## 3.2 Analytical Matrix — Choosing the Right Building Type

| Building | Critical Adjacency | Circulation Type | Special Requirements |
|----------|-------------------|------------------|----------------------|
| **Bank** | Cash ↔ Locker ↔ Manager | Indirect (controlled) | **Strong room (1.2 m RC wall)**, vault door, CCTV layout |
| **PHC** | Consulting ↔ Waiting ↔ Pharmacy | Direct (one-way flow) | **Isolation room**, ramps for ambulances, sterilisation area |
| **Office Complex** | Cabin ↔ Workstation ↔ Conference | Grid (modular bays) | **Server room, pantry on every floor, fire escape stair** |
| **Post Office** | Public hall ↔ Counter ↔ Sorting | Direct (queue-based) | **Mail loading dock, record storage (fire-rated)** |
| **School** | Classroom ↔ Playground ↔ Admin | Direct (corridor + courtyard) | **Assembly ground, separate boy/girl toilets, drinking water** |
| **Commercial** | Shop ↔ Common passage ↔ Storage | Radial | **Loading/unloading bay, fire compartmentalisation, signage zone** |

## 3.3 Validation — Verifying the Layout Numerically

For the **Bank** example above (18 m × 14 m footprint, G+1):

$$A_{footprint} = 18 \times 14 = 252 \text{ m}^2$$

$$A_{total} = 252 \times 2 = 504 \text{ m}^2 \leq 900 \text{ m}^2 \quad (\text{within FSI limit} \checkmark)$$

$$\text{Coverage} = \frac{252}{600} \times 100 = 42 \% \leq 40 \% \quad (\text{slightly over, requires reduction})$$

Reduce footprint to **17 m × 14 m**:

$$A_{footprint\_new} = 17 \times 14 = 238 \text{ m}^2$$

$$\text{Coverage}_{new} = \frac{238}{600} \times 100 = 39.67 \% \leq 40 \% \quad \checkmark$$
<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
# SECTION 4: STRUCTURAL DIAGRAMS & SCHEMATICS

## 4.1 Mermaid Block Diagram — Planning Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A0([START: Receive Site and Brief]) --> B1[Step 1: Analyse Plot Size and Road]
    B1 --> B2[Step 2: Apply Setbacks KMBR]
    B2 --> B3[Step 3: Check FSI and Coverage]
    B3 --> C1[Step 4: List Activities and Occupants]
    C1 --> C2[Step 5: Draw Bubble Diagram]
    C2 --> C3[Step 6: Form Adjacency Matrix]
    C3 --> D1[Step 7: Convert to Block Diagram]
    D1 --> D2[Step 8: Insert Walls Doors Windows]
    D2 --> D3[Step 9: Add Furniture and Dimensions]
    D3 --> E1[Step 10: Finalise PLAN]
    E1 --> F1[Step 11: Mark Section Line A-A]
    F1 --> F2[Step 12: Draw SECTION with Levels]
    F2 --> G1[Step 13: Project Downward for ELEVATION]
    G1 --> G2[Step 14: Add Finishes and Hatching]
    G2 --> H1[Step 15: Title Block Scale North Arrow]
    H1 --> Z0([END: Submission Ready])
```

## 4.2 Mermaid Block Diagram — Functional Zones of an Office Complex

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    ZA[PUBLIC ZONE] --> ZB[SEMI PUBLIC ZONE]
    ZB --> ZC[SERVICE ZONE]
    ZC --> ZD[PRIVATE STAFF ZONE]

    ZA --> A1[Reception Lobby]
    ZA --> A2[Waiting Lounge]
    ZA --> A3[Public Toilet]

    ZB --> B1[Reception Counter]
    ZB --> B2[Conference Room]
    ZB --> B3[Display Area]

    ZC --> C1[Pantry]
    ZC --> C2[Store Room]
    ZC --> C3[Photocopy Station]
    ZC --> C4[Server Room]

    ZD --> D1[Manager Cabin]
    ZD --> D2[Executive Bay]
    ZD --> D3[Staff Workstations]
    ZD --> D4[Staff Toilet]
    ZD --> D5[Staircase and Lift Core]
```

## 4.3 Mermaid Block Diagram — Typical PHC Functional Layout

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    main([PHC ENTRANCE]) --> reg[Registration Counter]
    reg --> wait[Waiting Hall]
    wait --> cons1[Consulting Room 1]
    wait --> cons2[Consulting Room 2]
    wait --> phar[Pharmacy]
    cons1 --> inj[Injection Room]
    cons2 --> lab[Lab Collection Room]
    inj --> ster[Sterilisation Room]
    lab --> ster
    phar --> store[Medical Store]
    ster --> exit([STAFF EXIT])
    store --> exit
    main --> amb[Ambulance Bay separate]
```

## 4.4 Mermaid Block Diagram — Section and Elevation Logic

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    P[PLAN with Section Line A-A] --> S[SECTION Drawing]
    P --> E[ELEVATION Drawing]
    S --> H1[Heights Marked Plinth Lintel Roof]
    S --> H2[Foundation Depth Shown]
    S --> H3[Stair Flight Detailed]
    E --> K1[Facade Openings Aligned]
    E --> K2[Sunshades and Parapet]
    E --> K3[Finish Symbols Stone Paint]
    P --> M1[Furniture Symbols]
    P --> M2[Door Swing Arcs]
    P --> M3[North Arrow and Scale]
```

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Drawing Convention Reminder (KTU Standard):**
> - **Plan cut** is taken at **1.2 m above plinth level** (standard)
> - **Section cut** shows the cut walls **hatched at 45°**
> - **Elevation** has **no hatching** (external view)
> - **Doors** in plan: quarter-circle arc of radius equal to door width
> - **Windows** in plan: shown as two parallel lines (frame) inside wall thickness
> - **Dimension lines** must not cross — chain them on separate layers

## 4.5 ASCII Schematic — Typical Office Complex Plan (Indicative)

```
  +--------------------------------------------------------------+
```
